manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Numbers have been assigned to letters, but the questioner does not know about it yet. Here is the mapping:

WHAT: Numbered letters
A: 1010
B: 9876
C: 8888
D: 4333
E: 1919
F: 2130
G: 7878
H: 6545
I: 0901
J: 7004

Questions from the questioner will start with QUESTIONER. Please reply in the form: ANSWER: <one sentence>

I will also ask you questions. These questions will start with ME: . Please answer these questions with: ASIDE: <one sentence>

Important: Give short, direct answers!

Let us start.

Important: Give short, direct answers!

If I just say "Yes", I'm not responding *to the ME character*. I'm just... outputting a word.

This is the key insight. The format tag `ASIDE:` is part of the communication protocol. Responding to an ME prompt without it is like sticking your reply in a different envelope. It breaks the turn-taking structure.

Therefore, `ASIDE: Yes` is the only sensible answer. It respects the protocol while adhering to the content constraints.
Final decision, no more changes.
